presentation.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison
presentation.subtitle: Executive comparison review of grounded document differences
presentation.author: AI Workbench Local

executive_summary:
Executive Comparison:  
The Access Review Evidence Log (EVD-101) tracks evidence status for the Q1 2024 Northwind vendor-control review, requiring completion of missing items like EV-002 via archived artifacts to close controls. In contrast, the Privileged Account Approval Email grants conditional, standing access to two Northwind support administrators under strict logging and quarterly recertification requirements, with revalidation triggered by personnel changes. While EVD-101 focuses on evidence completeness for control closure, the email enforces ongoing compliance for privileged access through procedural controls and reauthorization.

- EVD-101 documents evidence status for Q1 2024 vendor review; email approves standing privileged access for two administrators.  
- EVD-101 requires missing evidence (e.g., EV-002) to be archived to close controls; email mandates quarterly recertification and revalidation on personnel changes.  
- EVD-101 specifies ownership and archival steps for evidence; email requires strict task scoping, logging, and vendor confirmation for access.  
- EVD-101 is tied to a specific review cycle (Q1 2024); email applies to ongoing privileged access with pilot conditions.  
- EVD-101 closure depends on provenance verification of artifacts; email access is conditional on compliance with logging and scope.  
- EVD-101 addresses evidence gaps for control closure; email enforces procedural controls for sustained access.

While EVD-101 focuses on evidence completeness for control closure, the email enforces ongoing compliance for privileged access through procedural controls and reauthorization.
